Title: Unveiling the catalytical roles of three glycosyltransferases in triterpenoid saponins in quinoa

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C-3 glucuronidation branch of oleanane-type triterpene saponins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid from UDP-GlcA to the C-3 hydroxyl of oleanolic acid or hederagenin, producing 3-O-glucuronides such as calenduloside E
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms key precursor pool for downstream C-20/C-28 glycosylation and final quinoa triterpenoid saponins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: triterpenoid saponins (e.g., ginsenoside Ro and related quinoa saponins)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bitterness and defense against herbivores/microorganisms; pharmacological activities including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of C-3 glucuronidated triterpene saponin intermediates required for downstream saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in grains, low in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in grains, low in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse-grown quinoa grains versus 15-day-old seedlings; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided candidate selection; in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS; substrate/donor specificity assay; kinetic analysis; one-pot enzymatic synthesis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key entry glycosylation step, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient overexpression system in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA1169548; raw transcriptomic samples SAMN44078480-SAMN44078485; metabolomics (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqCsl2 was identified as a novel quinoa Csl-like enzyme highly expressed in grains. 2. It specifically used UDP-GlcA and catalysed C-3 glucuronidation of OA and HE in vitro. 3. Co-expression in tobacco confirmed in planta production of CE and supported reconstruction of downstream saponin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering or editing quinoa saponin content and for synthetic biology production of medicinal triterpene saponins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing activity may lower defensive bitter saponins in grain; increasing activity may elevate anti-nutritional bitterness while enhancing medicinal saponin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares 94.4% amino acid identity with previously reported CqCsl1; showed strict sugar donor specificity for UDP-GlcA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to C-3 glucuronidated triterpene substrates such as calenduloside E and chikusetsusaponin IVa, producing zingibroside R1 and ginsenoside Ro; can also glucosylate OA3-Ara before subsequent C-28 glucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: calenduloside E (CE); chikusetsusaponin IVa (Ch IVa); OA3-Ara; UDP-Glc; UDP-Xyl for CE
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor and, for some substrates, directly forms target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: triterpenoid saponins (e.g., ginsenoside Ro and related quinoa saponins)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense and bitterness; medicinal/pharmacological activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-3 sugar-chain extension and production of ZR1 and GRo, supporting sequential quinoa saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in grains, low in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in grains, low in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: grain versus seedling comparison; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; RNA-seq expression profiling; in vitro enzyme assays with LC-MS; kinetic analysis; sugar donor specificity assay; one-pot reaction; transient expression in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient overexpression system in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA1169548; raw transcriptomic samples SAMN44078480-SAMN44078485; metabolomics (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqUGT73FG1 was highly expressed in grains and catalysed addition of glucose to CE and Ch IVa. 2. It generated ZR1 from CE and GRo from Ch IVa in vitro and in planta. 3. It also glucosylated OA3-Ara, supporting a sequential mechanism in which C-3 sugar-chain elaboration precedes C-28 glucosylation for arabinosylated substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with CqCsl2 and CqUGT74BB3 for synthetic production of valuable triterpenoid saponins or manipulated to alter quinoa grain saponin composition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced activity may increase bitter/anti-nutritional saponins in edible grain while benefiting medicinal saponin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Displays donor flexibility with UDP-Glc and UDP-Xyl on CE, but only UDP-Glc on OA3-Ara; highest catalytic efficiency observed with Ch IVa

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C-28 glycosylation branch of oleanane-type triterpene saponins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the C-28 carboxyl group of C-3 modified triterpene substrates such as calenduloside E and zingibroside R1, producing chikusetsusaponin IVa and ginsenoside Ro; activity depends on prior C-3 glucuronidation or C-3 sugar-chain extension
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: calenduloside E (CE); zingibroside R1 (ZR1); UDP-Glc; can also use UDP-Gal, UDP-Ara, UDP-Rha to varying extents
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product or immediate precursor depending on substrate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: triterpenoid saponins (e.g., ginsenoside Ro and related quinoa saponins)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense and bitterness; medicinal/pharmacological activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-28 glucosylation of C-3 premodified triterpenes, enabling formation of major quinoa bidesmosidic saponins such as Ch IVa and GRo
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in grains, low in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in grains, low in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: grain versus seedling comparison; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided candidate selection; in vitro enzyme assays with LC-MS; kinetic analysis; sugar donor specificity assay; one-pot synthesis; transient expression in tobacco; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key downstream control step for C-28 glycosylation specificity, not explicitly claimed rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient overexpression system in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA1169548; raw transcriptomic samples SAMN44078480-SAMN44078485; metabolomics (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqUGT74BB3 was identified as the quinoa C-28 carboxyl glucosyltransferase acting on CE and ZR1. 2. It showed stronger catalytic efficiency toward ZR1 than CE and no activity on OA or OA3-Ara, demonstrating dependence on prior C-3 modification. 3. Molecular docking and mutagenesis established His13 and Asp104 as critical catalytic residues, and tobacco expression confirmed its role in producing Ch IVa and GRo.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for editing or stacking to modulate bitter grain saponins in quinoa and to biosynthetically produce medicinal oleanane saponins in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function may reduce defensive saponins in seeds; increased function may elevate bitterness/anti-nutritional traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: C-28 glycosylation occurs only when C-3 is modified with mono-glucuronidation or di-O-glycosylation; broad donor promiscuity with UDP-Glc > UDP-Gal > UDP-Ara >> UDP-Rha; catalytic dyad His13-Asp104 is essential
